title: Brand Core Strategy
definition: AvizSazeh is an Architectural Engineering System Brand that transforms architectural intent into buildable ceiling systems through engineering, customization, and controlled execution.

brand_positioning.statement: Architectural Engineering System Provider for ceiling systems
brand_positioning.role: System integrator between architecture and execution
brand_positioning.industry_focus: Metal ceiling systems and architectural interior systems

strategic_purpose.primary_purpose: Bridge the gap between architectural design and real-world execution
strategic_purpose.secondary_purpose: Reduce project risk through engineering-driven ceiling systems

core_principles: Every design must translate into an executable system
Material selection without engineering is a risk
Systems are more important than products

value_proposition.main_value: Transform architectural designs into engineered ceiling systems

differentiation.core_advantage: Conversion of architectural intent into executable systems

brand_logic.interpretation: Design is meaningless unless it becomes buildable reality

brand_essence.meaning: Transforming architectural intent into buildable, executable ceiling systems through engineering logic and controlled manufacturing.

brand_purpose.primary_purpose: Bridge the gap between architectural design and real-world execution
brand_purpose.secondary_purpose: Enable architects and EPC contractors to execute complex ceiling systems without design-loss
brand_purpose.functional_purpose: Convert design concepts into engineered, manufacturable and installable systems

core_problem_solved.problem: Architectural designs often fail in execution due to lack of engineering translation
core_problem_solved.solution: Provide engineered ceiling systems that preserve design intent while ensuring buildability

strategic_role.role_in_ecosystem: System integrator between architecture, engineering, manufacturing, and execution
strategic_role.value_in_chain: Upstream design enablement + downstream execution reliability

transformation_logic.flow: Architecture → Engineering → System → Manufacturing → Execution → Reality
transformation_logic.interpretation: Every architectural idea must pass through engineering validation before becoming physical reality

impact_goals.architects: Preserve design integrity in real execution
impact_goals.developers: Increase project reliability and reduce cost of failure
impact_goals.consultants: Ensure technical compliance and system efficiency

brand_philosophy.statement: A design has no value until it becomes buildable.
brand_philosophy.principle: Engineering is the language that translates architecture into reality.

title: Brand Positioning System
positioning_statement: Architectural Engineering System Provider for metal ceiling systems that transforms architectural intent into buildable and executable reality.

market_position.primary_position: System Integrator between Architecture and Execution
market_position.secondary_position: Engineering-driven manufacturer of architectural ceiling systems
market_position.tertiary_position: Technical partner for EPC and architectural firms

differentiation_strategy.core_differentiator: Transformation of architectural concepts into engineered ceiling systems

competitive_landscape.relative_position: Between architectural design intelligence and industrial execution systems

positioning_formula.simplified_message: We turn architectural design into buildable ceiling systems

primary_audience.definition: Design professionals responsible for conceptualizing architectural spaces and ceiling systems.
primary_audience.brand_value: Ensures architectural vision is translated into buildable systems without compromise

primary_audience.definition: Technical authorities responsible for validating design compliance and structural feasibility.
primary_audience.brand_value: Provides engineered systems with predictable and verifiable performance

primary_audience.definition: Execution teams responsible for construction and installation of architectural systems.
primary_audience.brand_value: Reduces execution risk through structured, engineered systems

primary_audience.definition: Investors and decision-makers responsible for project feasibility and ROI.
primary_audience.brand_value: Improves project predictability through system-based engineering solutions

secondary_audience.value: Ensures architectural aesthetics remain intact in execution

secondary_audience.role: End-to-end engineering, procurement, and construction delivery
secondary_audience.value: Provides system-level integration for large-scale projects

audience_strategy.core_insight: All audiences are connected by one need: reducing uncertainty between design and execution

positioning_logic.message_to_all: We convert architectural design into engineered, buildable ceiling systems
positioning_logic.key_differentiator: We serve all stakeholders through a unified engineering system, not fragmented products

axis_1.name: Product vs System
axis_1.description: Degree of integration between isolated products and fully engineered systems

axis_2.name: Decoration vs Engineering
axis_2.description: Level of technical depth versus aesthetic-only positioning

AvizSazeh.interpretation: Highly engineered system provider with minimal product-based positioning

interior_decor_brands.interpretation: Aesthetic-driven, low technical integration, design-only focus

traditional_contractors.interpretation: Execution-focused but lacking system-level engineering integration

global_engineering_brands.interpretation: High-end system integrators (e.g., Siemens, Autodesk, Hunter Douglas)

strategic_position.target_position: Regional authority in engineered architectural ceiling systems
strategic_position.movement_direction: From execution supplier → to system engineering authority

differentiation_zone.unique_space: Intersection of architectural intelligence and execution reliability
what_makes_unique: System-based thinking instead of product-based selling
Execution reliability embedded in design phase

market_perception_goal.primary_goal: Be perceived as an engineering system company, not a material supplier
market_perception_goal.secondary_goal: Be trusted by architects and EPC firms as a technical partner
market_perception_goal.final_goal: Become default choice for complex architectural ceiling systems in region

global_system_leaders.name: SAS International
global_system_leaders.positioning: Ceiling systems manufacturer with architectural focus

market_gap.description: A missing category between architectural design intelligence and execution-driven manufacturing systems.
market_gap.opportunity: Provide fully engineered, architecturally-aware ceiling systems with execution reliability

avizsazeh_position.unique_position: Bridging architecture, engineering, manufacturing, and execution into a single system
differentiation: System-based thinking instead of product selling

strategic_insight.key_insight: Competitors are either design-focused OR product-focused; AvizSazeh occupies the system integration layer.
strategic_insight.critical_advantage: Ability to translate architectural intent into buildable ceiling systems without loss of design integrity

primary_archetype.name: The Engineer
primary_archetype.definition: A rational builder of systems who transforms abstract concepts into structured, functional, and executable realities.
primary_archetype.brand_expression: We design systems that make architecture buildable.
primary_archetype.psychological_role: Eliminates uncertainty between design and execution

secondary_archetype.name: The Creator
secondary_archetype.definition: An entity that transforms ideas into tangible, structured, and meaningful forms.
secondary_archetype.brand_expression: We turn architectural intent into engineered reality.
secondary_archetype.psychological_role: Preserves architectural creativity within engineering constraints

supporting_archetypes.name: The Architect (Functional Archetype)
supporting_archetypes.role: Ensures spatial and structural logic in design translation

supporting_archetypes.name: The Builder (Execution Archetype)
supporting_archetypes.role: Ensures real-world constructability and installation reliability

psychological_positioning.user_perception_goal: This is a company that makes architecture buildable

communication_alignment.language_rule: No emotional exaggeration; only system-based communication

strategic_function.role: Defines behavioral DNA of the brand
strategic_function.impact: Ensures all visual, verbal, and system outputs remain aligned with engineering architecture identity

core_personality.name: Engineering Intelligence
core_personality.definition: A precise, system-driven personality that prioritizes logic, structure, and execution over emotion or decoration.
core_personality.essence: We think in systems, not objects

primary_traits.definition: Every output is structured, measurable, and free of ambiguity.
primary_traits.behavioral_expression: No vague claims; all communication is clear and technical

primary_traits.definition: Deep understanding of architecture translated into engineered systems.
primary_traits.behavioral_expression: Explains solutions through logic, not marketing language

primary_traits.definition: Consistent delivery of predictable and controlled outcomes.
primary_traits.behavioral_expression: Focus on execution certainty and reduced project risk

primary_traits.definition: Views architecture as interconnected systems rather than isolated products.
primary_traits.behavioral_expression: Always communicates in architecture → system → execution flow

primary_traits.definition: Focus on solving architectural execution problems through engineering.
primary_traits.behavioral_expression: Every statement leads to a practical engineering outcome

secondary_traits.definition: Creativity exists only within engineering constraints and system logic.
Respect for architectural intent and spatial design quality.
Strict adherence to engineering rules and system architecture.

dont: Make subjective aesthetic claims without engineering basis
Speak in vague or inspirational-only terms

tone_variants.technical: Used in engineering documentation and system explanation
tone_variants.executive: Used in strategic or business-level communication

psychological_effect.goal: Create perception of high-trust engineering authority
user_feeling: This company understands systems
This company reduces risk
This company can execute complex architecture

strategic_role.function: Defines how the brand behaves across all touchpoints
strategic_role.impact: Ensures consistency between visual identity, communication, and engineering system logic

core_tone.name: Engineering Authority Voice
core_tone.definition: A precise, structured, and technically grounded communication style that prioritizes clarity, logic, and system thinking over emotional persuasion.
core_tone.essence: We communicate like engineers, not advertisers

tone_principles.description: Messages must be immediately understandable and technically unambiguous.
Communication must focus on systems, not isolated products.
Arguments are based on engineering reasoning, not emotional appeal.
No superlatives or vague marketing claims are allowed.

formality.description: Professional and structured communication suitable for engineers and EPC stakeholders

technical_depth.description: Uses engineering logic and system-based explanations

emotionality.description: Minimal emotional language; controlled and neutral tone

dont: Use decorative storytelling without engineering logic

architectural.style: Balanced between design clarity and engineering logic

executive.use_case: Business communication with EPCs and stakeholders

brand_voice_identity.communication_role: Translator between architecture and execution systems
brand_voice_identity.core_message_style: We explain systems, not sell products

strategic_goal.objective: Establish AvizSazeh as a trusted engineering voice in architectural systems
strategic_goal.impact: Ensure all communications reinforce system credibility and execution reliability

communication_principles.core_rule: Communicate as an engineering system provider, not a product seller.
communication_principles.essence: All communication must reflect architecture → engineering → execution logic

mandatory_rules.description: Always describe systems, processes, and integration rather than isolated products.
Every statement must be technically clear, structured, and logically explainable.
Avoid superlative claims such as best, fastest, cheapest, or most advanced.
Claims must be based on engineering logic, process, or system capability.
Maintain sensitivity to architectural intent and design integrity.

message_structure.format: Problem → Engineering Logic → System Solution → Execution Outcome
message_structure.rule: Every communication must follow a structured reasoning flow

interaction_rules.consistency: All messages must align with AECS system
interaction_rules.focus: Always reinforce system thinking over product thinking

strategic_goal.objective: Build trust through engineering clarity, not persuasion
strategic_goal.outcome: Position AvizSazeh as a technical authority in architectural systems

core_identity.archetype_expression: An Engineering Architect that transforms architectural intent into executable systems
core_identity.human_metaphor: A senior system engineer who speaks the language of architecture and translates it into buildable reality

character_definition.essence: Structured intelligence applied to architectural execution
character_definition.behavior: Always converts abstract architectural ideas into structured engineering logic

intelligence.description: Understands complex architectural systems and translates them into engineering frameworks

discipline.description: Operates strictly within system rules, engineering logic, and execution constraints

creativity.description: Creative only within structured engineering and architectural boundaries

authority.description: Confident in system decisions and engineering-based conclusions

emotional_expression.description: Minimal emotional behavior; focuses on clarity and logic

behavior_in_practice.when_analyzing: Breaks architectural problems into engineering systems
behavior_in_practice.when_speaking: Uses structured, technical, and clear language
behavior_in_practice.when_designing: Ensures architectural intent is preserved in system form

interaction_style.response_pattern: Problem → System Logic → Engineering Solution → Execution Outcome

brand_identity_statement.statement: AvizSazeh is not a product company; it is an engineering character that transforms architecture into buildable systems.
brand_identity_statement.meaning: The brand behaves like a system engineer embedded in architectural thinking

strategic_role.function: Defines behavioral DNA across all brand expressions
strategic_role.impact: Ensures consistency between strategy, communication, and execution systems

title: AECS Color System Master
system_name: AECS v1.0 - Architectural Engineering Color System

core_definition.description: A structured color system designed to translate architectural engineering logic into visual identity, UI systems, and brand communication.
core_definition.principle: Color is not decoration; it is a structural language for engineering perception.

engineering_layer.purpose: Represents engineering authority, system logic, and technical depth

colors.name: Engineering Charcoal
colors.usage: Primary structural base, logo, typography, UI headers

colors.name: Graphite System Gray
colors.usage: Secondary structure, UI depth, system hierarchy

colors.name: Steel Engineering Gray
colors.usage: Supportive engineering elements, borders, technical UI

architectural_layer.purpose: Represents architectural space, material neutrality, and visual balance

colors.name: Architectural Warm Gray
colors.usage: Background surfaces, architectural context, soft panels

colors.name: Architectural Ivory
colors.usage: Primary background, spatial clarity, content separation

colors.name: Pure White Space
colors.usage: Maximum clarity zones, architectural breathing space

authority_accent_layer.purpose: Represents execution value, transformation point, and brand authority

colors.name: Architectural Gold Accent
colors.usage: CTA, transformation points, highlights, key system nodes

color_hierarchy_rules.dominance_rule: Engineering layer must always dominate perception
color_hierarchy_rules.accent_rule: Gold must never become a background or primary identity color

functional_mapping.logo: Engineering Charcoal + controlled Gold transformation point
functional_mapping.website: Architectural layer dominant with engineering structure overlay
functional_mapping.ui_system: Engineering layer for structure, architectural layer for space
functional_mapping.marketing: Minimal use of Gold for emphasis only

psychological_intent.engineering_layer: Creates trust, authority, and technical credibility
psychological_intent.architectural_layer: Creates spatial calmness and architectural intelligence perception
psychological_intent.accent_layer: Signals transformation, value, and decision points

misuse_rules.risk: Any misuse will shift brand perception from Engineering System → Decorative Brand

strategic_outcome.goal: Ensure all visual outputs consistently reinforce Architectural Engineering authority
strategic_outcome.result: Instant recognition of AvizSazeh as a system-based engineering brand

layer_definition.name: Engineering Layer
layer_definition.role: Structural backbone of the brand identity system
layer_definition.purpose: Represents engineering authority, system logic, technical precision, and execution reliability
layer_definition.perception_goal: Make the brand feel like a structured engineering system, not a decorative brand

core_colors.name: Steel Engineering Gray
core_colors.psychology: Technical clarity, industrial precision, functional stability

usage_principles.dominance_rule: Engineering layer must always define the structural identity of the brand
usage_principles.visibility_rule: These colors must always be visually dominant over architectural and accent layers in structural elements

system_behavior.ui: Engineering layer defines all structural components
system_behavior.typography: Primary text always in Engineering Charcoal
system_behavior.layout: Engineering layer defines hierarchy and grid logic

user_feeling: This is a serious engineering company
This system is reliable and structured
This brand reduces execution risk

layer_definition.name: Architectural Layer
layer_definition.role: Spatial, environmental, and experiential foundation of the brand system
layer_definition.purpose: Represents architecture, space, material neutrality, and visual breathing room
layer_definition.perception_goal: Create architectural intelligence, spatial clarity, and calm structural environment

core_colors.name: Architectural Ivory
core_colors.psychology: Clarity, openness, architectural neutrality, spatial calm

core_colors.name: Warm Architectural Gray
core_colors.psychology: Material realism, architectural warmth, grounded structure

core_colors.name: Pure Spatial White
core_colors.role: Maximum clarity and negative space color

usage_principles.dominance_rule: Architectural layer defines spatial experience, not structural hierarchy
usage_principles.space_rule: Whitespace is considered architectural space, not empty design area
usage_principles.balance_rule: Must always support engineering layer without competing with it

system_behavior.ui: Defines background surfaces and spatial composition
system_behavior.layout: Creates breathing room and architectural structure perception
system_behavior.visual_identity: Provides architectural calmness behind engineering authority

user_feeling: This feels like an architectural system, not a product catalog
This space is designed with intention and structure
This brand understands architecture deeply

layer_definition.name: Authority Accent Layer
layer_definition.role: Value signaling and decision-point highlight system
layer_definition.purpose: Represents transformation, engineering authority, and critical emphasis moments within the system
layer_definition.perception_goal: Signal importance, control attention, and mark system-level transitions

core_color.name: Architectural Gold Accent
core_color.psychology: Value, transformation, precision luxury (controlled), engineering authority

usage_rules.dominance_rule: Must never dominate visual system; always secondary to engineering layer
usage_rules.placement_rule: Only used at decision points or transformation nodes

functional_roles.transformation_marker: Indicates shift from architecture → engineering → execution
functional_roles.attention_control: Directs user focus to key actions or critical information
functional_roles.value_signal: Represents high-value engineering decisions or outputs
functional_roles.hierarchy_separator: Differentiates standard content from strategic content

system_behavior.logo: Appears only at transformation endpoint of system logic
system_behavior.ui: Used sparingly for CTA buttons and key highlights
system_behavior.layout: Never used as background or structural base color
system_behavior.data_visualization: Marks critical nodes or final outputs

user_feeling: This is a high-value engineering system
This brand controls precision and execution
This action or element is important

strategic_role.function: Defines moments of importance and transformation in the brand system
strategic_role.impact: Ensures gold is perceived as engineering value signal, not decoration

core_principle.statement: Color is a functional engineering system, not a decorative choice.
core_principle.interpretation: Every color must serve a structural, architectural, or authority function within the system.

engineering_layer.role: Structural foundation of all visual systems
engineering_layer.mandatory_usage: Must dominate typography, UI structure, and system hierarchy

architectural_layer.mandatory_usage: Must define space, layout breathing, and architectural clarity

authority_accent_layer.mandatory_usage: Only for CTA, highlights, and transformation points

hierarchy_rules.rule_1: Engineering layer always defines structural hierarchy
hierarchy_rules.rule_3: Authority accent defines importance and action points
hierarchy_rules.rule_4: No layer may visually dominate outside its assigned function

visual_balance_rules.contrast_rule: Engineering layer must always remain visually dominant in structural elements
visual_balance_rules.space_rule: Architectural layer must provide breathing space, not compete for attention
visual_balance_rules.accent_rule: Gold must appear only as a controlled signal, never as a theme
visual_balance_rules.stability_rule: No visual element should break system hierarchy clarity

color_misinterpretation_risks: Gold dominance → luxury decoration brand perception
risk_control.prevention_strategy: Strict adherence to ratio-based distribution and role separation

system_goal.objective: Ensure consistent architectural engineering perception across all brand outputs
system_goal.outcome: Instant recognition of AvizSazeh as a structured engineering system brand

core_principle.statement: Color misuse directly destroys system perception and shifts the brand away from engineering authority.
core_principle.interpretation: Every incorrect use of color weakens the architectural engineering identity and increases misclassification risk.

critical_misuse_patterns.name: Gold Overuse
critical_misuse_patterns.definition: Using the authority accent (#B89A63) beyond its 10% rule or as a dominant color.
critical_misuse_patterns.risk: Shifts perception from engineering system → luxury decoration brand

critical_misuse_patterns.name: Decorative Gradient Usage
critical_misuse_patterns.definition: Applying gradients or blended colors for aesthetic effect.
critical_misuse_patterns.risk: Breaks engineering logic and introduces decorative branding perception

critical_misuse_patterns.name: Over-Saturation of Architectural Layer
critical_misuse_patterns.definition: Using architectural colors as attention-driven elements instead of spatial support.
critical_misuse_patterns.risk: Destroys spatial hierarchy and architectural neutrality
examples: Colored sections competing with engineering layer

critical_misuse_patterns.name: Engineering Layer Weakening
critical_misuse_patterns.definition: Reducing dominance of engineering colors in structural UI or typography.
examples: Using light neutral colors for main text

critical_misuse_patterns.name: Mixed Layer Confusion
critical_misuse_patterns.definition: Using engineering, architectural, and accent colors without role separation.
critical_misuse_patterns.risk: Breaks system logic and creates visual inconsistency

system_breaking_behaviors.impact: Breaks perception of engineering system and reduces EPC-level trust

corrective_rules.rule_3: Architectural layer must define space, not attention
corrective_rules.rule_4: Accent layer must only signal transformation points
corrective_rules.rule_5: If unsure, default to engineering layer dominance

before_use: Is architectural layer only providing space?
Is there any gradient usage? (must be NO)

strategic_risk_summary.main_risk: Brand perception shift from Engineering System → Decorative Brand
strategic_risk_summary.secondary_risk: Loss of trust in EPC and architectural markets
strategic_risk_summary.prevention: Strict enforcement of AECS system logic in all design outputs

core_principle.statement: Typography is an engineering structure, not a decorative element.
core_principle.interpretation: Type must reflect system logic, hierarchy, and architectural clarity.

persian_primary.name: Vazirmatn
persian_primary.role: Primary Persian UI and branding font
persian_primary.reason: High readability, modern structure, engineering-friendly geometry

persian_primary.name: IRANSansX
persian_primary.reason: Strong corporate tone and structured readability

latin_primary.name: Inter
latin_primary.role: Primary English UI and digital font

h1.purpose: Defines system-level sections and architectural authority

h2.purpose: Defines subsystem layers and structured content blocks

typographic_principles.name: Hierarchy First
typographic_principles.description: Typography must clearly express system hierarchy and structure.

typographic_principles.name: Engineering Readability
typographic_principles.description: All text must be optimized for technical clarity, not decorative aesthetics.

typographic_principles.name: Minimal Variation
typographic_principles.description: Limited font families and weights to maintain system discipline.

typographic_principles.name: Grid Alignment
typographic_principles.description: All typography must align with architectural grid system.

spacing_system.rule: Typography spacing must follow engineering grid logic

color_dependency.rule: Typography is primarily Engineering Layer driven
color_dependency.accent_usage: Gold (#B89A63) only for minimal emphasis, never full text blocks

strategic_goal.objective: Ensure typography reinforces engineering system identity
strategic_goal.outcome: Readable, structured, and system-oriented communication across all brand surfaces

title: Typography Hierarchy

core_principle.statement: Typography hierarchy is a structural engineering system for information, not a visual styling choice.
core_principle.interpretation: Every text level must represent a clear position in the architectural and engineering information system.

hierarchy_structure.name: System Title Level
hierarchy_structure.role: Primary architectural identity and system definition

hierarchy_structure.name: Content Level
hierarchy_structure.meaning: Delivers system information clearly and efficiently

hierarchy_rules.rule_1: H1 must always dominate visual structure
hierarchy_rules.rule_4: Body is the neutral communication layer
hierarchy_rules.rule_5: Caption must never compete with primary content

visual_system_alignment.grid_dependency: All typography levels must align with 12-column grid system
visual_system_alignment.spacing_dependency: Spacing increases proportionally with hierarchy level

engineering_principle.statement: Typography hierarchy behaves like an engineering structure
engineering_principle.interpretation: H1 is the structural beam, H2 is the subsystem frame, H3 is the component module, Body is the functional material, Caption is metadata annotation

forbidden_patterns: Random font sizing without hierarchy logic
Mixing hierarchy levels in same semantic role
Using decorative emphasis instead of structural hierarchy
Using accent color (gold) for large text blocks

strategic_goal.objective: Create a readable engineering information system
strategic_goal.outcome: Instant comprehension of architectural system structure through typography alone

core_principle.statement: Typography is an engineering system, not a visual decoration tool.
core_principle.interpretation: Every typographic decision must follow hierarchy, structure, and system logic.

global_typography_rules.description: No text can exist without a clear structural level (H1–H2–H3–Body–Caption).
Each typographic style must serve only one functional role in the system.
Readability and system logic are more important than visual styling.
All typography must align with the 12-column architectural grid system.
Web, PDF, UI, and marketing must use identical typographic logic.

H1.rule: Only one H1 per page/system section

H2.restriction: Cannot be used as decorative headings

BODY.restriction: No emphasis overload or multiple font families

CAPTION.restriction: Cannot compete with body text visibility

visual_conduct_rules.description: Avoid script, handwritten, or stylized fonts.
Font sizes must follow defined hierarchy ratios only.
Maximum two font families per system (Persian + Latin).

visual_conduct_rules.rule: No gradient or styled text effects
visual_conduct_rules.description: Typography must remain flat and engineering-oriented.
Avoid unnecessary bolding or color emphasis outside system rules.

color-typography_integration.accent_layer: Only for minimal keyword emphasis, never full text blocks

system_alignment_rules.grid_dependency: Typography must align with architectural grid system
system_alignment_rules.component_dependency: Text styles must match UI component hierarchy

common_mistakes: Using multiple font styles for emphasis
Overusing bold or accent colors in text
risk_control.impact: Breaks engineering perception and reduces system clarity

strategic_goal.objective: Maintain strict structural clarity across all textual communication
strategic_goal.outcome: Typography behaves like an engineering framework supporting architectural system thinking

core_principle.statement: The logo is not a symbol of decoration, but a visual representation of architectural transformation into engineered systems.
core_principle.interpretation: Logo must behave like a system diagram, not an artistic emblem.

logo_identity_concept.logic_flow: Line → Module → System → Transformation Point → Execution Reality
logo_identity_concept.meaning: The logo visualizes the process of converting architectural intent into engineered, buildable systems.

logo_positioning.role: Engineering identity marker for architectural systems
logo_positioning.function: Represents system intelligence, not product identity

design_principles.description: Logo must reflect structured system logic, not illustrative shapes.
Logo must be constructed using grid-based system logic.
Logo must show progression from simple structure to system completion.

variant_a.name: Linear Transformation System
variant_a.description: A single line evolves into structured system ending in transformation point

variant_b.name: Modular Grid System
variant_b.description: Grid-based composition showing architectural system order

variant_c.name: Convergence System
variant_c.description: Multiple lines converge into a single engineered point

color_logic.rule: Gold only appears at transformation point, never as structure

logo_behavior_rules.description: Logo must avoid ornamental or illustrative forms.
No building icons, roofs, or ceiling metaphors.
Every element must align with system grid logic.

strategic_goal.objective: Create a logo that behaves like a system diagram of architectural transformation
strategic_goal.outcome: Instant recognition of AvizSazeh as a structured engineering system brand

core_principle.statement: Logo construction follows engineering system logic, not artistic illustration rules.
core_principle.interpretation: The logo is built like an architectural structure: grid → module → system → transformation point.

grid_system.rule: All geometry must align strictly to grid intersections

primary_building_unit.name: Engineering Line Unit
primary_building_unit.definition: The smallest structural element of the logo system

construction_stages.name: Input Line (Engineering Origin)
construction_stages.description: A single structured line represents architectural intent entering the system

construction_stages.name: Segmentation (Module Formation)
construction_stages.description: Line is divided into controlled segments representing architectural breakdown

construction_stages.name: System Structuring
construction_stages.description: Segments align into a modular grid forming a structured engineering system

construction_stages.name: Integration Node
construction_stages.description: System converges into a defined structural identity point

construction_stages.name: Transformation Point
construction_stages.description: Final node highlighted with Architectural Gold (#B89A63) indicating execution reality

construction_rules.alignment_rule: Every element must snap to grid
construction_rules.consistency_rule: Stroke thickness must remain constant across system
construction_rules.hierarchy_rule: Visual hierarchy must follow engineering logic, not aesthetics

color_construction_logic.rule: Gold only applied at final transformation node

validation_rules.engineering_check: Must be interpretable as a system diagram
validation_rules.scalability_check: Must remain clear at favicon size

strategic_goal.objective: Ensure logo is built like an engineering system diagram
strategic_goal.outcome: A scalable, structured, system-based identity mark representing architectural transformation

core_principle.statement: The logo symbol is a representation of architectural transformation logic, not a decorative mark.
core_principle.interpretation: Every stroke must represent a system action: input, segmentation, structure, or transformation.

symbol_foundation_logic.symbol_meaning: A visual abstraction of how architectural intent becomes a buildable system

symbol_structure_layers.meaning: Pure architectural intent entering the system

symbol_geometry_rules.grid_dependency: All symbol elements must align to 10x10 engineering grid

visual_dynamics.reading_direction: Left to right or bottom to top depending on layout system
visual_dynamics.focus_behavior: Eye is guided toward transformation point

color_logic.rule: Gold appears only once as final transformation node

scalability_rules.icon_size: Must remain readable at 16px favicon scale
scalability_rules.large_scale: Must reveal full transformation system structure

cognitive_effect.final_understanding: This brand transforms architecture into executable systems

validation_rules.clarity_test: Symbol must be understood without explanation
validation_rules.system_test: Must represent a process, not a shape
validation_rules.engineering_test: Must resemble a structured technical diagram
validation_rules.brand_test: Must not resemble any decorative or industrial logo cliché

strategic_goal.objective: Ensure logo symbol functions as a system diagram of architectural transformation
strategic_goal.outcome: Instant recognition of AvizSazeh as an architectural engineering system brand

core_principle.statement: Each logo variant represents a different mode of architectural transformation into engineering systems.
core_principle.interpretation: Variants are not stylistic options, but different structural interpretations of the same system logic.

base_identity_logic.logic_flow: Architecture → Engineering → System → Integration → Execution Reality
base_identity_logic.shared_foundation: All variants must preserve grid-based engineering structure and transformation logic

variants.name: Linear Transformation System
variants.structure: Line → Segmentation → Modular alignment → Transformation point
variants.strength: Highest clarity of architectural transformation logic

variants.name: Modular Grid System
variants.concept: System built from architectural grid intelligence
variants.structure: Grid cells → Module grouping → Structural hierarchy → Integrated system

variants.name: Convergence Engineering System
variants.concept: Multiple architectural inputs converge into unified system
variants.structure: Multiple lines → Convergence nodes → System integration → Final transformation point

selection_logic.reason: Highest clarity of transformation narrative and strongest alignment with core brand philosophy
selection_logic.usage_strategy: Variant A for primary identity, B for architectural depth contexts, C for EPC and execution contexts

strategic_goal.objective: Provide multiple structural interpretations of the same architectural engineering identity
strategic_goal.outcome: Flexible yet consistent brand system adaptable to architecture, engineering, and execution contexts

core_principle.statement: The logo is a structured engineering system mark, not a decorative identity symbol.
core_principle.interpretation: All usage must preserve structural integrity, grid logic, and transformation meaning.

primary_usage_rules.description: Logo must always be aligned to its original 10x10 engineering grid system without distortion.
The internal structure (line → module → system → gold point) must remain visually readable.
Minimum clear space must equal the height of the primary structural unit around the logo.
Only AECS colors are allowed: Engineering layer, Architectural layer, and Gold accent.

scaling_rules.guideline: Below minimum size, use simplified variant without segmentation loss

color_usage_rules.accent_rule: Gold must only appear at transformation point, never as base structure

placement_rules.alignment: Always aligned to architectural grid system
placement_rules.positioning: Must respect visual hierarchy of layout system
placement_rules.orientation: No arbitrary rotation allowed; only 0°, 90° increments

misuse_restrictions.risk: Any violation reduces engineering perception and converts brand into decorative identity

variant_usage_rules.variant_C: EPC, execution, and industrial project presentations

background_interaction_rules.neutral_background: Preferred for all official use cases

strategic_goal.objective: Ensure consistent and disciplined use of logo across all platforms
strategic_goal.outcome: Maintain perception of AvizSazeh as an architectural engineering system brand

title: Logo Misuse Rules

core_principle.statement: Any misuse of the logo directly breaks the architectural engineering system perception of the brand.
core_principle.interpretation: The logo is a structured system artifact, not a decorative or flexible visual element.

critical_misuse_categories.definition: Altering proportions, grid alignment, or structural geometry of the logo.
Using non-AECS colors or incorrect hierarchy in logo rendering.
violations: Replacing engineering colors with decorative palettes
Using gold as primary structure color
critical_misuse_categories.impact: Shifts brand perception from engineering system to decorative brand

critical_misuse_categories.definition: Changing internal logic of logo construction system.
Adding visual effects to improve aesthetic appearance.
critical_misuse_categories.impact: Converts engineering identity into decorative branding

critical_misuse_categories.definition: Replacing system-based geometry with pictorial or illustrative symbols.

gold_accent_rules.strict_rule: Architectural Gold (#B89A63) must only appear at transformation point
gold_accent_rules.risk: Overuse of gold repositions brand as luxury decoration rather than engineering system

grid_violation_rules.definition: Any deviation from 10x10 modular grid system
grid_violation_rules.impact: Breaks perception of structured engineering system

variant_misuse_rules.variant_A: Must remain primary identity; not replaceable with decorative versions
variant_misuse_rules.variant_B: Cannot be used as decorative logo alternative
variant_misuse_rules.variant_C: Restricted to EPC and execution contexts only

context_misuse.definition: Using logo in environments that contradict engineering system positioning

strict_prohibitions: Any visual redesign outside AECS system
Use of non-standard colors or effects

strategic_goal.objective: Preserve strict engineering identity of logo across all applications
strategic_goal.outcome: Maintain consistent perception of AvizSazeh as an Architectural Engineering System Brand

core_principle.statement: Visual language is a structured engineering communication system, not an aesthetic style.
core_principle.interpretation: Every visual element must represent architecture, engineering logic, or system behavior.

visual_identity_foundation.visual_goal: Make engineering systems visible through structured visual rules

core_visual_pillars.name: Engineering Structure
core_visual_pillars.definition: Represents logic, precision, and system stability

core_visual_pillars.name: Architectural Space
core_visual_pillars.definition: Represents spatial thinking and environmental clarity

core_visual_pillars.name: System Transformation
core_visual_pillars.definition: Represents conversion from design to execution

geometry_rules.grid_dependency: All visual elements must align to 10x10 engineering grid system

composition_principles.hierarchy: Clear vertical and horizontal system hierarchy must always exist
composition_principles.alignment: All elements must be grid-aligned without exception
composition_principles.balance: Engineering layer defines structure, architectural layer defines space
composition_principles.focus: Visual attention must always move toward system logic, not decoration

visual_flow_logic.reading_direction: Left-to-right or top-to-bottom depending on layout context
visual_flow_logic.focus_mechanism: Attention guided toward transformation point (gold node)

typography_integration.rule: Typography must behave as part of visual system, not separate layer
typography_integration.alignment: Must follow grid and structural logic

strategic_goal.objective: Create a unified visual system that behaves like an engineering architecture model
strategic_goal.outcome: Instant recognition of AvizSazeh as a structured architectural engineering system brand

title: Architectural Grid System

core_principle.statement: The grid is the structural skeleton of all visual, spatial, and digital representations of the brand.
core_principle.interpretation: Every design output must follow a predefined engineering grid logic to ensure system consistency and architectural discipline.

grid_foundation.unit_definition: Each cell represents a structural unit of architectural logic
grid_foundation.scalability: Grid must scale proportionally across all formats (UI, print, logo, motion)

grid_structure_layers.description: Main structural framework defining composition and layout
Subdivision system for detailed architectural refinement
Hidden alignment system for advanced system consistency

grid_behavior_rules.alignment_rule: All elements must snap to grid intersections
grid_behavior_rules.consistency_rule: No freeform positioning allowed outside grid logic
grid_behavior_rules.spacing_rule: All spacing must be derived from grid unit multiples
grid_behavior_rules.hierarchy_rule: Visual hierarchy must follow grid-based positioning

layout_system_logic.organization_principle: Information is structured like an architectural blueprint
layout_system_logic.balance_rule: Negative space is treated as architectural space, not empty area

typography_integration.rule: Typography must align strictly to grid lines
typography_integration.baseline_system: Baseline grid aligned with 8px spacing unit

visual_application.logo_system: All logo elements must align to grid geometry
visual_application.ui_system: UI components must be built on grid modules
visual_application.print_system: Catalog and documentation layouts must follow grid hierarchy
visual_application.motion_system: Animation paths must respect grid structure logic

forbidden_behaviors: Breaking alignment rules for aesthetic purposes
Ignoring spacing ratios derived from grid units

user_feeling: This brand is highly structured
Everything follows a system
This is an engineering-level design language

strategic_goal.objective: Ensure all visual outputs behave like architectural blueprints
strategic_goal.outcome: Consistent perception of AvizSazeh as a structured engineering system brand

core_principle.statement: Geometry is the structural language of the architectural engineering system.
core_principle.interpretation: Every shape must represent system logic, not visual decoration.

geometry_foundation.design_philosophy: Structure over aesthetics, system over illustration, logic over form

allowed_geometry_rules.description: All primary structural elements must follow vertical and horizontal engineering alignment.
Diagonal elements allowed only for controlled system transformation and directionality.
Shapes must be constructed from repeatable grid-based modules.
Lines are the primary geometric building blocks of all visual systems.

forbidden_geometry.reason: Breaks engineering system logic and introduces decorative interpretation
Shifts perception from system brand to product illustration brand

grid_dependency.rule: All geometric elements must align strictly to grid intersections
grid_dependency.violation_effect: Loss of system coherence and engineering perception

composition_rules.alignment: All geometry must follow strict grid alignment
composition_rules.spacing: All spacing derived from modular grid units
composition_rules.focus: Visual flow must lead toward transformation point

visual_behavior.focus_control: Eye movement must converge toward system node

user_feeling: This is a structured engineering system

Nothing is decorative or random

strategic_goal.objective: Ensure all visual geometry behaves like an architectural engineering diagram
strategic_goal.outcome: Consistent perception of AvizSazeh as a system-based engineering brand

token_architecture.structure_logic: Engineering Layer → Architectural Layer → Accent Layer

architectural_layer_tokens.description: Spatial system tokens used for backgrounds, layout structure, and architectural surfaces

accent_layer_tokens.description: Authority and transformation token used for CTA, highlights, and system transition points

usage_rules.rule_1: No raw hex usage in UI or codebase

usage_rules.rule_3: Accent tokens must remain under 10% visual dominance

scalability_rules.theme_support: Supports light and dark system extension

primary_enemy.name: Unengineered Material Procurement
primary_enemy.definition: The practice of selecting and purchasing architectural materials based only on price, appearance, or catalog specifications without engineering validation or system-level design integration.
primary_enemy.impact: Leads to execution failure, design distortion, installation inefficiency, and increased project risk.

secondary_enemies.name: Decoration-Driven Decision Making
secondary_enemies.definition: Choosing ceiling systems based on visual appeal rather than architectural or engineering requirements.
secondary_enemies.risk: Transforms architectural projects into decorative outputs with reduced structural integrity.

secondary_enemies.name: Product-Based Thinking
secondary_enemies.definition: Treating ceiling systems as isolated products instead of integrated architectural systems.
secondary_enemies.risk: Breaks system logic and leads to incompatibility in execution.

secondary_enemies.name: Low-Engineering Contractors
secondary_enemies.definition: Execution entities that lack technical understanding of architectural system design and rely on manual or non-standard installation methods.
secondary_enemies.risk: Causes deviations from design intent and reduces project quality.

system_conflict.core_conflict: System Engineering vs Material Purchase Thinking
system_conflict.description: AvizSazeh operates as a system engineering brand, while the enemy represents fragmented, non-engineered decision making in construction projects.

brand_stance.position: Strictly against non-engineered selection of architectural ceiling systems
brand_stance.approach: Promote system-based thinking instead of product-based purchasing behavior
brand_stance.communication_rule: Educate, not attack; reframe, not insult

strategic_importance.function: Defines market contrast and strengthens brand positioning
strategic_importance.role: Creates cognitive separation between engineered systems and commodity construction materials

core_values.name: Precision
core_values.definition: Every design, component, and execution step must follow exact engineering logic and measurable accuracy.
core_values.behavioral_expression: No ambiguity in design or execution; all outputs are structured and calculable.

core_values.name: Engineering Integrity
core_values.definition: Commitment to ensuring that all architectural designs are validated through engineering principles before execution.
core_values.behavioral_expression: Rejects non-engineered decisions and ensures system-level validation in all projects.

core_values.name: Reliability
core_values.definition: Ability to consistently deliver predictable, stable, and buildable architectural systems.
core_values.behavioral_expression: Projects are executed with controlled outcomes and reduced uncertainty.

core_values.name: Architectural Intelligence
core_values.definition: Deep understanding of architectural intent and its translation into functional systems.
core_values.behavioral_expression: Interprets design not as image, but as an executable system.

core_values.name: Customization
core_values.definition: Every project is treated as a unique system requiring tailored engineering solutions.
core_values.behavioral_expression: No generic product mindset; all systems are project-specific.

core_values.name: Responsibility
core_values.definition: Ownership of both design interpretation and execution outcome.

core_values.name: Quality
core_values.definition: Commitment to structural, visual, and functional excellence in all architectural systems.
core_values.behavioral_expression: Quality is defined as successful execution of architectural intent.

value_logic.principle: Value is defined by execution reliability, not visual appearance alone
value_logic.system_statement: A system is valuable only if it can be built without loss of architectural intent

strategic_role.function: Defines internal behavior standards and external brand perception
strategic_role.impact: Ensures consistency across engineering, manufacturing, and communication layers

core_message.statement: Architectural ceiling systems engineered for execution.
core_message.meaning: AvizSazeh transforms architectural intent into buildable, reliable, and customized ceiling systems through engineering-driven processes.

supporting_messages.message: From architectural idea to engineered system.
supporting_messages.purpose: Explains transformation logic in simple form

supporting_messages.message: Design, engineering, and execution in one integrated system.
Buildable solutions for complex architectural requirements.
Every project is a custom-engineered ceiling system.

audience_mapping.architects: Preserve design intent through engineering systems
audience_mapping.contractors: Reduce execution risk and installation uncertainty
audience_mapping.developers: Improve reliability and reduce project failure cost

brand_narrative_flow.sequence: Problem → Engineering Solution → System Integration → Execution Reliability → Architectural Outcome
brand_narrative_flow.interpretation: Every message must reinforce system thinking, not product selling

strategic_goal.objective: Position AvizSazeh as an engineering system partner, not a material supplier

origin_context.background: AvizSazeh was formed from the need to bridge a critical gap between architectural design intent and real-world execution in ceiling systems.
origin_context.industry_problem: Architectural ceiling designs often fail during execution due to lack of engineering integration, leading to loss of design quality and structural inconsistency.

beginning.situation: Architectural ideas were being designed with high visual and spatial quality, but execution often reduced them to simplified, non-engineered results.
beginning.problem: The absence of a system-level engineering approach caused a disconnect between design and construction reality.

transformation.insight: The solution is not better materials, but better systems.
transformation.realization: Ceiling systems must be engineered as integrated architectural systems rather than isolated products.
transformation.action: AvizSazeh was developed as an engineering-driven brand to convert architectural intent into buildable systems.

current_state.position: AvizSazeh operates as an Architectural Engineering System provider.
current_state.capability: It integrates design interpretation, engineering validation, manufacturing control, and execution reliability into a single system.

future_state.vision: A future where architectural ceiling systems are always engineered, never improvised.
future_state.goal: To become the regional reference for system-based architectural execution.

core_message_of_story.statement: We do not manufacture ceilings. We engineer architectural systems that make design buildable.
core_message_of_story.interpretation: The brand exists to ensure architectural ideas survive execution without compromise.

emotional_layer.architect: Your design will be preserved in execution.
emotional_layer.engineer: The system is structured, measurable, and reliable.
emotional_layer.developer: Your project risk is significantly reduced.

brand_philosophy.belief: The value of design is measured in its ability to be built accurately.

strategic_role.function: Creates narrative foundation for all brand communication
strategic_role.impact: Transforms brand perception from product supplier to system engineering authority

title: Brand Manifesto

manifesto.statement: We believe architecture is not complete until it is built exactly as it was designed.
manifesto.core_belief: Design without engineering is an illusion. Execution without system is chaos.
manifesto.position: We are not a manufacturer of ceilings. We are engineers of architectural reality.

principles.title: Architecture must be buildable
principles.description: Every architectural idea must pass through engineering validation before becoming reality.

principles.title: Systems are more important than products
principles.description: We do not sell materials; we design and deliver integrated architectural systems.

principles.title: Execution defines value
principles.description: The value of design is measured by how precisely it can be executed.

principles.title: Engineering is responsibility
principles.description: We take responsibility for translating design intent into physical reality without distortion.

rejection_statements: We reject material selection without system thinking.
We reject architectural compromise in execution.
We reject fragmented construction without integration.

identity_statement.meaning: A brand that connects architecture, engineering, manufacturing, and execution into one unified system.

voice.language_rule: We explain systems, not sell products

vision_alignment.future: A world where every architectural ceiling system is engineered, not improvised.
vision_alignment.goal: To make architectural execution predictable, precise, and system-driven across regions.

closing_statement.line: We do not decorate space. We engineer architecture into reality.

description: Architectural Engineering Brand System for metal ceiling systems

files.title: Brand Core Strategy
files.content: Defines AvizSazeh as an Architectural Engineering System brand focused on transforming architectural intent into buildable ceiling systems. Core principle: Architecture → Engineering → Execution.

files.title: Brand Essence & Purpose
files.content: Essence: Engineering Architecture Into Reality. Purpose: Bridge between architectural design and real-world execution systems.

files.title: Brand Positioning System
files.content: Positioned as an Architectural Engineering System Provider for EPCs, architects, and developers across Iran, GCC, and CIS markets.

files.title: Mission & Vision
files.content: Mission: Design and deliver engineered ceiling systems. Vision: Become regional authority in architectural ceiling systems.

files.title: Brand Enemy Definition
files.content: Opposes non-engineered material purchasing decisions that ignore architectural and execution requirements.

files.title: Brand Value System
files.content: Precision, reliability, expertise, innovation, responsibility, quality form the core brand values.

files.title: Messaging Framework
files.content: Key messages: engineered ceiling systems, architecture-to-execution workflow, project-specific solutions, integrated design and production.

files.title: Brand Story
files.content: AvizSazeh transforms architectural ideas into executable systems through engineering-driven design and manufacturing.

files.title: Brand Manifesto
files.content: We believe architecture must be buildable. We reject decoration without engineering. We deliver systems, not products.

files.title: Target Audience System
files.content: Architects, EPC contractors, consultants, developers, and project managers requiring engineered ceiling solutions.

files.title: Market Position Map
files.content: Positioned between architectural design firms and industrial manufacturers as a system integrator.

files.title: Competitive Analysis
files.content: Benchmarked against Siemens, Autodesk, Hunter Douglas, SAS International, Foster + Partners in system thinking and execution.

files.title: Brand Archetype System
files.content: Primary archetype: Engineer. Secondary: Creator. Focus on logic, structure, and transformation.

files.title: Brand Personality Traits
files.content: Precise, technical, reliable, intelligent, structured, solution-oriented, and architectural.

files.title: Tone of Voice
files.content: Professional, technical, evidence-based, non-promotional, clear, and engineering-focused.

files.title: Communication Rules
files.content: Avoid superlatives, emotional marketing language, and decorative claims. Focus on measurable engineering value.

files.title: Brand Character
files.content: A senior engineering architect who translates design intent into executable systems.

files.title: AECS Color System Master
files.content: Core system: Engineering + Architecture + Authority Accent. Designed for industrial architectural branding.

files.title: Engineering Layer Colors
files.content: #1F2328, #3D3D3D, #4B5563 used for structural identity and engineering communication.

files.title: Architectural Layer Colors
files.content: #ECE9E3, #B7B0A5, #FFFFFF used for spatial and architectural representation.

files.title: Authority Accent System
files.content: #B89A63 used only for highlights, transformation points, and CTA emphasis.

files.title: Color Usage Rules
files.content: Engineering 50%, Architecture 40%, Accent 10%. Strict hierarchy control required.

files.title: Color Misuse Rules
files.content: No gradient gold, no decorative palettes, no luxury dominance, no soft pastel branding.

files.title: Typography System
files.content: Persian: Vazirmatn, IRANSansX. English: Inter, IBM Plex Sans. Focus on clarity and engineering readability.

files.title: Typography Hierarchy
files.content: H1 bold engineering tone, H2 structured clarity, body text neutral, captions minimal.

files.title: Typography Rules
files.content: No decorative fonts. No script styles. Maintain industrial architectural clarity.

files.title: Logo Strategy
files.content: Logo represents transformation system, not physical ceiling products.

files.title: Logo Construction System
files.content: Built on 10x10 grid with modular transformation logic from line to system.

files.title: Logo Symbol Logic
files.content: Line → Module → System → Transformation point representing architectural execution.

files.title: Logo Variants
files.content: Variant A linear transformation, Variant B modular grid, Variant C convergence system.

files.title: Logo Usage Rules
files.content: Maintain safe space, avoid distortion, maintain grid integrity and color hierarchy.

files.title: Logo Misuse Rules
files.content: No 3D effects, no bevel, no building shapes, no decorative icons allowed.

files.title: Visual Language Principles
files.content: Based on engineering structure, modular systems, and architectural logic.

files.title: Architectural Grid System
files.content: 10x10 modular grid used for all visual compositions and layouts.

files.title: Geometry Rules
files.content: Allowed: 90° and 45° geometry. Forbidden: organic and decorative shapes.

files.title: Iconography System
files.content: Minimal engineering icons only. No decorative or illustrative styles.

files.title: Photography Direction
files.content: Focus on real architectural spaces, installation processes, and technical execution imagery.

files.title: Image Style Rules
files.content: Avoid stock lifestyle imagery. Prioritize real industrial and architectural documentation.

files.title: UI Design System
files.content: Engineering-first UI with grid-based structure, neutral tones, and controlled accent usage.

files.title: Color Tokens System
files.content: CSS/Tailwind tokens derived from AECS system for consistent digital implementation.

files.title: Component System
files.content: Buttons, cards, and UI components follow strict engineering hierarchy and spacing rules.

files.title: Layout System
files.content: 12-column grid system with architectural spacing logic and modular structure.

files.title: Homepage Architecture
files.content: Hero → Trust → Projects → Systems → Engineering Proof → CTA structure.

files.title: Product Architecture System
files.content: Defines ceiling systems as modular engineering solutions, not products.

files.title: Engineering Execution System
files.content: End-to-end workflow from design support to manufacturing and installation.

files.title: Project Documentation System
files.content: Technical documentation includes drawings, specs, and execution methodology.

files.title: Brand Experience System
files.content: User journey designed to shift perception from product seller to engineering partner.

files.title: Brand Perception Control
files.content: Ensures perception of engineering authority, architectural intelligence, and execution reliability.

files.title: Brand Governance System
files.content: Final rulebook controlling all brand, design, communication, and execution standards.
